meta.reportLabel: Thu - ESCALATION, not resolution: crude climbs another rung to WTI ~$88 / Brent ~$96 on a 12th strike night, a Saudi-area tanker hit and an explicit Trump red line (any Hormuz-ship strike = the US destroys 'one bridge or power plant'). The day's domestic mover is Australian: June jobs BLEW OUT at +76.3k (vs +15k), re-arming AUD and NEUTRALISING the AUD/NZD short into Q2 CPI (Jul 29). One honest correction: the July FOMC is NOT a coin-flip - CME now prices ~83% hold (~16% hike), so September is the live meeting. CAD/JPY LONG (HIGH) is the cleanest trade; AUD/JPY UPGRADED to LONG; USOIL/XAU/XAG stay LONG; JPY the clean loser at 163.1. Today's binary is the ECB (12:15Z).
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: Escalation, not resolution: a 12th strike night, a Saudi-area tanker hit and an explicit Trump red line pushed crude to the cycle high (WTI ~$88.2, Brent ~$96). The oil premium is at its widest - CAD is the clearest winner, JPY the clean loser at a 40-year low (~163.1). The domestic story is Australian: a +76.3k jobs blowout re-armed AUD and neutralised the AUD/NZD short into Q2 CPI (Jul 29). Correction to the week's tape: the July FOMC is a hawkish hold (~83%), not a coin-flip - September (~50%) is the live meeting.
meta.nextBigEvent: The ECB decision (Thu Jul 23, 12:15Z) is today's binary - a >99% hold where Lagarde's September signal is the whole EUR trade - ahead of a dense run: FOMC Jul 28-29 (hawkish hold ~83%), AU Q2 CPI Jul 29, BoE Jul 30 and BoJ Jul 31, all under a still-escalating Hormuz oil premium.

dailyRead: Escalation, not resolution - the oil premium climbed another rung. US forces struck a 12th consecutive night, a tanker was hit ~70nm off Al Shuqaiq (Saudi Arabia), and Trump drew an explicit red line - any Iranian strike on a Hormuz ship now means the US will destroy 'ONE BRIDGE OR POWER PLANT,' with Iran vowing to hit US-linked regional energy in return. Crude pushed to the strongest print of the cycle: WTI ~$88.2 (+1.5%), Brent ~$96 (+2.5%). The day's domestic mover is Australian: June jobs BLEW OUT at +76.3k (vs +15k exp), unemployment steady 4.4%, participation 67% - a hot beat that re-arms AUD and collapses the AUD/NZD short's catalyst asymmetry into Q2 CPI (Jul 29). Net: JPY still the clean oil loser (USD/JPY ~163.1, MoF verbal), CAD the clearest winner with WTI at $88, and gold/silver bid ($4,119 / $59.5). One honest correction to the week's tape: the July FOMC is NOT a coin-flip - CME now prices ~83% hold (~16% hike), so September is the live meeting, not July. Cleanest lean flips to CAD/JPY LONG (HIGH) - oil-winner vs oil-loser at the cycle's strongest crude; AUD/NZD neutralised to RANGE on the jobs blowout; AUD/JPY upgraded to LONG (MED); USOIL / XAU / XAG stay LONG. Today's binary is the ECB (12:15Z): a near-certain hold where Lagarde's September signal is the entire EUR trade, and spot 163.1 remains deep in the MoF strike zone with three more CB binaries in eight days.

today.headline: June jobs BLEW OUT at +76.3k (vs +15k exp), unemployment steady 4.4%, participation up to 67% - a hot beat.
today.read: The blowout re-arms AUD and ends its run as the board's passenger: it revives the Aug 11 RBA hike tail, NEUTRALISES the AUD/NZD short from HIGH to RANGE, flips AUD/CAD to RANGE and upgrades AUD/JPY to LONG. But unemployment only held and Q2 CPI (Jul 29) is the real decider - treat the AUD trades as provisional.

today.headline: Crude pushed to the cycle high (WTI ~$88.2 / Brent ~$96) as escalation deepened - CAD's terms-of-trade premium is at its widest this month.
today.read: CAD is the clearest oil winner and CAD/JPY LONG (HIGH) is the day's cleanest trade - oil-winner vs oil-loser at the strongest crude of the cycle. The hawkish leg is oil, not data (June core soft, trim 1.8%), so a ceasefire is the one thing that unwinds it.

today.headline: Honest correction: CME now prices the July FOMC as ~83% hold / ~16% hike - the ~46.5% seen Jul 13 has faded.
today.read: July is a hawkish hold, not a coin-flip; September (~50%) is the live meeting. The escalation still hands the dollar a haven bid and crude at $88 keeps the inflation leg, so DXY firms - but it stays mid-pack, losing to hawkish-CPI GBP and hot-CPI NZD.

today.headline: ECB decides today (12:15Z) - a >99% hold with a September hike now near-fully priced as energy re-fires the inflation channel.
today.read: The entire EUR trade is whether Lagarde endorses or dodges the September pricing in the press conference. A ceasefire would remove the energy argument; HICP near target could let her lean neutral.

today.headline: No change - still the clean oil loser, USD/JPY ~163.1 at a 40-year low with FinMin Katayama verbal only.
today.read: The cycle-high oil bill hits the energy importer hardest and spot is deep in the MoF strike zone. Offset builds: Tokyo trend CPI at 1.9% arms the BoJ for Jul 31. Size every XXX/JPY carry-long for a violent MoF snap.

today.headline: No change - the hot 4.1% Q2 CPI holds and oil at $88 keeps renewing the petrol driver.
today.read: Back-to-back RBNZ hikes into Aug 19 stay live. Still narrow (ex-fuel 2.9%) - and the AUD/NZD short that rode NZD's edge is now neutralised after the AU jobs blowout; the swing moves to AU Q2 CPI Jul 29.

today.headline: No change - the hot June CPI (3.6%/core 3.7%) keeps the BoE hawks armed into Jul 30.
today.read: The fuel-driven beat hardens both hike dissents. Powerful but narrow (motor fuels the top contributor) - watch for the MPC signalling it looks through fuel at Jul 30, which would unwind the GBP-hawk trades.

strength.tag: Haven bid; July hike a tail
strength.drivers: Core CPI 2.6% (Jun, flat m/m), headline 3.5%; NFP +57k with unemployment 4.2%; Fed 3.50-3.75% on a hawkish hold; CME July-hike odds now ~16% (hold ~83%), Sept ~50%; DXY firm on escalation haven flows.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Escalation hands the dollar a haven bid and crude at $88 keeps its inflation leg - but honest correction: CME now prices ~83% hold / ~16% July hike (the ~46.5% seen Jul 13 has faded). So the Jul 28-29 FOMC is most likely a hawkish hold; September (~50%) is the live meeting. Warsh's 'prices are too high' plus a re-spiking oil bill is the hawkish engine. Risk: a ceasefire deflates the scare; a soft Aug 12 CPI unwinds it.

strength.drivers: Q2 CPI 4.1% y/y (vs 4.0% exp) and 1.5% q/q, a two-year high above the RBNZ's own 3.9% call; OCR 2.50% after the Jul 8 hike; unemployment 5.3%; sectoral factor model 2.7%.
strength.forward: 🦅 The 4.1%/1.5% double beat keeps back-to-back hikes live into Aug 19; oil at $88 renews the petrol +27.5% driver rather than unwinding it. Still narrow (ex-fuel 2.9%, sectoral model 2.7%). Risk: RBNZ officials explicitly discount fuel; a late ceasefire; a risk-off flush hits high-beta NZD.

strength.drivers: June CPI 3.6% y/y (vs 3.4% exp, up from 3.4%) with core 3.7% (vs 3.5%), motor fuels the top contributor; unemployment 4.6%, DMP wages 4.2%; BoE 3.75% on a hawkish 7-2 hold with two hike votes.
strength.forward: 🦅 June CPI at 3.6% with core 3.7% overshot, motor fuels the top contributor - the same oil pass-through under NZD. It hardens both hike dissents and the Bank's own path (CPI past 3.25% into Q4) into Jul 30 (BoE + MPR). Risk: the beat is fuel-led and the MPC looks through it; gilt jitters cap the upside; a risk-off flush hits high-beta GBP.

strength.drivers: June CPI 2.8% (from 3.2%) with CPI-trim 1.8%, median 1.9%, common 2.6% - all cooled; BoC held 2.25% on Jul 15 for a sixth straight time; jobs +18.2k, unemployment 6.5%; WTI at the cycle high ~$88.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Crude's push to WTI $88 / Brent $96 - the strongest of the cycle - makes CAD once again the board's cleanest oil beneficiary; the terms-of-trade premium is at its widest this month. The offset is domestic: June CPI cooled to 2.8% with all three BoC core gauges falling (trim 1.8%), so the hawkish leg is oil, not data. Risk: a genuine ceasefire removes the premium outright; soft core caps the upside at the next BoC (Sep).

strength.drivers: June HICP 2.8% (from 3.2%) with core at 2.5%; unemployment 6.3%; ECB deposit rate 2.25% after the Jun 11 hike, meeting-by-meeting guidance; Jul 23 hold >99% priced; a September hike now near-fully priced.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Today's ECB (12:15Z) is a >99% hold; a September hike is now nearly fully priced and the energy resurgence keeps HICP risks tilted up. The entire EUR trade is whether Lagarde endorses or dodges the September pricing in the press conference. Risk: HICP near target lets Lagarde lean neutral; a ceasefire removes the energy argument.

strength.drivers: June employment +76.3k (vs +15k exp) with participation at a one-year-high 67%, unemployment steady 4.4%, underemployment up to 6.5%; trimmed mean 3.6% (May), headline 4.0% above band; RBA held 4.35% in June.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ June employment surged +76.3k (vs +15k exp) with participation at a one-year-high 67% - a hot beat that revives the Aug 11 RBA hike tail and ends AUD's run as the board's passenger. Tempering it: unemployment only held (didn't fall), underemployment rose to 6.5%, and Q2 CPI on Jul 29 is the true swing factor. Risk: a soft Q2 CPI Jul 29 unwinds the jobs pop; a ceasefire risk-on melt-up lifts high-betas broadly.

strength.drivers: Tokyo core 1.6% (Jun) with the fuel-stripped trend gauge at 1.9%; headline 1.5% below the 2% target; unemployment 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26; BoJ 1.00% after the Jun 16 hike; USD/JPY ~163.1.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Crude at $88 hits the energy-importing yen hardest; USD/JPY sits ~163.1, a fresh 40-year low, with FinMin Katayama repeating the government 'stands ready' - verbal only so far (the record 11.73tn yen Apr/May intervention failed to hold within six weeks). The offset is real - Tokyo trend CPI at 1.9% hands the BoJ ammunition at Jul 31 - but spot is deep in the strike zone. Risk: an MoF strike snaps the yen higher in minutes; a hawkish BoJ Jul 31 breaks the carry.

symbols.risk: USD/JPY ~163.1 is a live MoF strike zone; a hawkish BoJ Jul 31; RBNZ discounts the fuel print

catalysts.event: Escalation deepens - 12th strike night; Saudi-area tanker hit; Trump red line on Hormuz
catalysts.note: The single biggest two-way risk: escalation, not resolution, drove crude to the cycle high. A ceasefire would unwind every oil-linked leg in a session.

catalysts.event: US strikes + naval blockade - Hormuz traffic sharply reduced
catalysts.note: Every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY and gold; Iran vows to hit US-linked regional energy if infrastructure is struck.
A 40-year low; FinMin Katayama verbal-only so far; the record 11.73tn yen Apr/May precedent failed to hold within six weeks.
Hold at 2.25% is >99% priced; a September hike is near-fully priced - Lagarde's endorse-or-dodge is the entire trade for EUR.
Unemployment steady 4.4%, participation 67%; revives the Aug 11 RBA hike tail and re-arms AUD.
Warsh round 2; correction - July is a hawkish hold (~83%), not a coin-flip; September (~50%) is the live one.
The AUD's true swing factor; decides the Aug 11 RBA and re-opens the AUD/NZD short if soft.
Dissent count after the hot June CPI, plus new forecasts.
Tokyo trend CPI at 1.9% gives the hawks ammunition with spot ~163.1 in the strike zone.
Q2 CPI Jul 29 and the +76.3k jobs blowout decide it.
The oil pass-through test for the Fed's September call.
Live for a back-to-back hike after the 4.1% Q2 print; oil at $88 renews the petrol driver.

geopolitics: Escalation, not resolution - the oil trade is one-way up and the premium is at the cycle high. US forces struck a 12th consecutive night; a tanker was hit ~70nm off Al Shuqaiq (Saudi Arabia); and Trump drew an explicit line - any Iranian strike on a Hormuz ship now triggers a US strike on 'ONE BRIDGE OR POWER PLANT,' with Iran vowing to retaliate against US-linked regional energy assets. Both sides are downplaying peace talks. Shipping through the strait - normally ~20% of world oil flow - is severely disrupted, and crude pushed to the strongest print of the cycle: WTI ~$88.2 (+1.5%), Brent ~$96 (+2.5%), US pump prices above $4/gallon. FX transmission: (1) CAD is the clearest winner - WTI $88 / Brent $96 put its terms-of-trade premium at its widest this month, though June core (trim 1.8%) keeps the hawkish leg oil-driven; CAD/JPY LONG (HIGH) is the cleanest expression. (2) The JPY is the clean loser - the cycle-high oil bill hits the importer hardest and USD/JPY sits ~163.1, a 40-year low deep in the MoF strike zone, even as Tokyo trend 1.9% builds the BoJ's case for Jul 31. (3) The USD gets a haven bid, but the honest correction is that July-hike odds are only ~16% (hold ~83%) - September (~50%) is the live meeting; DXY firms but stays mid-pack. (4) The domestic story is Australian - a +76.3k jobs blowout re-arms AUD, neutralises AUD/NZD to RANGE and flips AUD/CAD to RANGE; Q2 CPI Jul 29 is now the swing. (5) The EUR's September-hike case is near-fully priced as energy renews the inflation argument into today's ECB. (6) Gold ~$4,119 and silver ~$59.5 stay bid. Every oil-linked leg keys off the same binary - and today it broke further up.
